docs.title: A Production Use Case for the Multigen Multi-Agent Orchestration Framework
Table of Contents
docs.text:
1. Executive Overview
  2. Business Problem
  3. Solution Architecture
  4. System Components
  5. Graph Design
  6. Agent Catalogue
  7. Reasoning Mechanisms
  8. Data Flow
  9. Step-by-Step Walkthrough
  10. Expected Outputs
  11. Failure Modes and Recovery
  12. Observability and Monitoring
  13. MCP Integration
  14. Deployment Architecture
  15. Performance Characteristics
  16. Extending the Use Case

docs.title: 1. Executive Overview
docs.text:
Use Case: Autonomous M&A Due Diligence for the acquisition of NovaSemi Technologies by GlobalTech Semiconductor Corp

What it demonstrates: - A 11-node directed graph with back-edges (cycles) for iterative refinement - 5 expert agents executing in parallel (saving ~3× wall-clock time) - Automatic reflection loops when any agent's confidence score falls below threshold - A fan-out with 3 market analysis variants (bear/base/bull) merged via highest-confidence consensus - Human-in-the-loop interruption gate after risk synthesis for CFO review - Circuit breaker protecting the compliance agent with automatic fallback routing - Real-time state persistence in MongoDB readable without touching Temporal internals - Full observability via OpenTelemetry spans and Prometheus metrics - Control via MCP tools from Claude or any MCP-capable host

Deal parameters: | Parameter | Value | |-----------|-------| | Target company | NovaSemi Technologies | | Acquirer | GlobalTech Semiconductor Corp | | Sector | Semiconductor / Fabless IC Design | | Target revenue | $340M | | Target EBITDA | $78M | | Deal rationale | RISC-V IP portfolio + automotive MCU market + 183-person ML accelerator team |

docs.title: 2. Business Problem
docs.text:
Traditional M&A due diligence is:

  • Slow: 4–12 weeks involving 50+ people across legal, financial, technical, and strategy teams
  • Siloed: Each workstream works in isolation; risk signals don't cross-pollinate until the final week
  • Inconsistent: Quality depends heavily on individual analyst expertise and availability
  • Expensive: Tier-1 investment bank fees range $2–15M for a mid-market transaction
  • Error-prone: Manual aggregation of 200+ page reports introduces synthesis errors

Target outcome with Multigen: | Metric | Traditional | Multigen | |--------|------------|---------| | Time to first risk report | 2–3 weeks | 45–90 minutes | | Consistency | Variable | Deterministic per model version | | Parallelism | Sequential workstreams | All 5 domains in parallel | | Re-analysis cost | High (human time) | Near-zero (re-run graph) | | Audit trail | Email chains + PDFs | Immutable Temporal event history + MongoDB | | Human touchpoints | 50+ people | 1 CFO gate (configurable) |

docs.title: 3. Solution Architecture
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        CLIENT LAYER                                   │
│  Claude (MCP)  ·  Python SDK  ·  REST API  ·  run.py CLI             │
└────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │ HTTP POST /workflows/run
┌────────────────────────────▼────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ORCHESTRATOR (FastAPI :8000)                        │
│                                                                       │
│  workflow.py router   →  DSL parser  →  capability validator          │
│  graph.py router      →  signal/query proxy to Temporal               │
│                                                                       │
│  Kafka publish: topic=flow-requests                                   │
└────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │
                    ┌────────▼────────┐
                    │  Apache Kafka   │  topic: flow-requests
                    └────────┬────────┘
                             │
┌────────────────────────────▼────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  TEMPORAL WORKER (temporal_worker.py)                  │
│                                                                       │
│  Registered workflows:  ComplexSequenceWorkflow, GraphWorkflow        │
│  Registered activities: agent_activity, tool_activity,                │
│                         persist_node_state_activity,                  │
│                         create_agent_activity                         │
└────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │
┌────────────────────────────▼────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              GRAPH WORKFLOW ENGINE (GraphWorkflow)                     │
│                                                                       │
│  BFS execution queue (deque)                                          │
│  CircuitBreakerRegistry  ·  ReasoningStrategies  ·  GraphTelemetry   │
│                                                                       │
│  Per-node execution:                                                  │
│  1. Interrupt check   2. Prune/skip    3. Circuit breaker             │
│  4. Tool activities   5. Agent activity 6. Confidence score           │
│  7. Reflection inject 8. MongoDB write  9. Edge evaluation            │
└──────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────┘
       │ activity calls                                      │ CQRS write
┌──────▼──────┐                                    ┌────────▼────────┐
│  AGENT POOL  │                                    │    MONGODB       │
│              │                                    │                  │
│ DataIngest   │                                    │ multigen.        │
│ Financial    │                                    │ graph_state      │
│ Legal        │                                    │ (node outputs)   │
│ Technical    │                                    │                  │
│ Market       │                                    │ multigen.        │
│ Culture      │                                    │ capabilities     │
│ RiskSynth    │                                    └─────────────────┘
│ Valuation    │
│ Compliance   │   Each agent calls
│ Critique     │   OpenAI GPT-4o
│ ExecSummary  │   with structured
│              │   JSON output mode
└──────────────┘

docs.title: 4.1 Multigen Orchestrator
docs.text: FastAPI service on port 8000. Accepts workflow DSL, validates agents against the Capability Directory, publishes to Kafka, and proxies graph control signals/queries to Temporal.

docs.title: 4.2 Capability Directory Service
docs.text: FastAPI service on port 8001. Registry of all available agent names, versions, and descriptions. Orchestrator validates every step's agent field against this registry before dispatching.

docs.title: 4.3 Temporal Workflow Engine
docs.text: Durable execution engine. Every node execution is an Activity with configurable retry policy. The GraphWorkflow maintains BFS state, circuit breakers, and signal buffers as in-memory workflow instance state — fully recoverable on worker restart.

docs.title: 4.4 Kafka
docs.text: Decouples the REST API from the Temporal worker. The orchestrator publishes to flow-requests; the worker consumer starts the Temporal workflow. Dead-letter queue on flow-dead-letter.

docs.title: 4.5 MongoDB
docs.text: CQRS read model. After each node completes, persist_node_state_activity upserts {workflow_id, node_id, output, updated_at} into multigen.graph_state. External dashboards, audit systems, and the SDK's get_state() read from here without touching Temporal.

docs.title: 4.6 MCP Server
docs.text: Thin adapter layer. Exposes every orchestrator capability as an MCP tool. Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, and custom agents can start, control, and inspect workflows through natural tool calls.

docs.title: 5.2 Edge Conditions
docs.text: Source Target Condition Type ingest financial (none) Parallel fan-out ingest legal (none) Parallel fan-out ingest technical (none) Parallel fan-out ingest market_primary (none) Parallel fan-out ingest culture (none) Parallel fan-out financial risk_synthesis (none) Convergence legal risk_synthesis (none) Convergence technical risk_synthesis (none) Convergence market_primary risk_synthesis (none) Convergence culture risk_synthesis (none) Convergence risk_synthesis valuation (none) Sequential valuation financial valuation.confidence < 0.85 Back-edge (cycle) valuation compliance valuation.confidence >= 0.85 Forward (guarded) compliance exec_summary (none) Sequential

docs.title: 5.3 Graph Settings
docs.text:
max_cycles = 6          # valuation↔financial cycle capped at 6 total executions
circuit_breaker:
  trip_threshold       = 3     # 3 consecutive failures → OPEN
  recovery_executions  = 4     # auto-recover after 4 total graph iterations

docs.title: 6. Agent Catalogue
docs.text: Agent Role LLM Prompt Focus Output Keys Reflection Threshold DataIngestionAgent Normalise raw company data Schema extraction, data quality company_name, revenue_usd_m, data_quality_issues 0.70 FinancialAnalystAgent P&L, DCF, capital structure Investment bank financial analysis revenue_cagr_3yr, ebitda_margin, dcf_valuation_usd_m, red_flags 0.80 LegalDueDiligenceAgent Contracts, IP, litigation M&A legal counsel ip_ownership_clean, litigation_exposure_usd_m, legal_risk_rating 0.75 TechnicalDueDiligenceAgent Tech stack, debt, security CTO-level tech assessment tech_stack_modernity_score, technical_debt_severity, integration_complexity 0.75 MarketAnalysisAgent TAM, competition, moat Strategy consultant market analysis tam_usd_b, market_share_pct, competitive_moat, strategic_fit_score 0.78 CultureFitAgent Leadership, retention, alignment Organisational psychologist cultural_alignment_score, estimated_attrition_pct, integration_difficulty 0.72 RiskSynthesizerAgent Master risk register Chief Risk Officer synthesis overall_risk_rating, deal_breakers, top_5_risks, recommendation_to_proceed 0.82 ValuationAgent DCF + CCA + precedent + LBO Investment banking MD recommended_bid_range_usd_m, ev_to_ebitda_multiple, recommended_premium_pct 0.85 ComplianceAgent Antitrust, CFIUS, regulatory Regulatory affairs partner hsr_filing_required, eu_phase_2_risk, estimated_clearance_months — CritiqueAgent Quality critic (all domains) Senior quality reviewer critique, identified_gaps, improved_output, confidence — ExecutiveSummaryAgent Board recommendation memo Managing Director, M&A headline_recommendation, deal_rationale, headline_numbers, next_steps 0.88 FallbackComplianceAgent Conservative compliance fallback Static conservative estimates All compliance fields, regulatory_risk_rating=high —

docs.title: 7.1 Confidence-Based Reflection Loops
docs.text:
Every agent returns a confidence field (0.0–1.0). The graph engine checks this after each node:

confidence = extract_confidence(output)   ← checks: confidence, score, quality, certainty keys

if confidence < reflection_threshold and reflection_count < max_reflections:
    → inject CritiqueAgent node at FRONT of queue (priority lane)
    → CritiqueAgent receives: {subject: prior_output, instruction: "critique and improve"}
    → CritiqueAgent returns: {critique, identified_gaps, improved_output, confidence}
    → improved output replaces prior in context
    → downstream nodes see the improved version

Example chain for financial analysis: 

financial runs → confidence=0.71 (< 0.80 threshold)
  → financial__reflect_1 (CritiqueAgent) runs immediately
  → critique: "DCF assumptions lack working capital detail"
  → improved_output with confidence=0.87
  → risk_synthesis receives financial__reflect_1 output (improved)

docs.title: 7.2 Fan-Out / Parallel Hypothesis Testing
docs.text:
The bear/base/bull market analysis fan-out:

fan_out signal received:
  group_id = "market_scenarios"
  consensus = "highest_confidence"
  nodes = [market_bear, market_base, market_bull]

asyncio.gather runs all 3 in parallel as Temporal activities
  market_bear:  TAM=$12B, growth=3%, moat=weak,     confidence=0.74
  market_base:  TAM=$18B, growth=8%, moat=moderate, confidence=0.88  ← selected
  market_bull:  TAM=$28B, growth=15%, moat=strong,  confidence=0.71

select_consensus(strategy="highest_confidence") → market_base result
context["market_scenarios"] = market_base output

This prevents anchoring bias — no single market analyst defines the entire view.

docs.title: 7.3 Iterative Valuation Cycle
docs.text:
The back-edge valuation → financial implements a financial re-analysis cycle:

valuation runs → confidence=0.73 (< 0.85 threshold)
  edge: valuation → financial fires (condition: valuation.confidence < 0.85)
  financial re-runs with updated context (now has risk_synthesis data too)
  financial__reflect_1 may run again if still low confidence
  valuation re-runs → confidence=0.91 ✓
  edge: valuation → compliance fires (condition: valuation.confidence >= 0.85)

Cycle guard (max_cycles=6) ensures this terminates.

docs.title: 7.4 Human-in-the-Loop Gate
docs.text:
After risk_synthesis completes:

# Sent via SDK or MCP tool:
await client.interrupt(workflow_id)

# CFO reviews:  GET /workflows/{id}/state/risk_synthesis
# CFO approves: await client.resume(workflow_id)
# CFO rejects:  await client.prune_branch(workflow_id, "valuation")  ← kills all downstream

The workflow pauses at the next node boundary. All in-flight Temporal activities complete normally. State is fully preserved — no re-execution of completed nodes on resume.

docs.title: 7.5 Circuit Breaker — Compliance Agent
docs.text:
ComplianceAgent failure 1 → CB records failure (consecutive=1)
ComplianceAgent failure 2 → CB records failure (consecutive=2)
ComplianceAgent failure 3 → CB TRIPS → state=OPEN
  → no more calls to ComplianceAgent
  → fallback_agent="FallbackComplianceAgent" detected on node
  → FallbackComplianceAgent injected at queue front
  → returns conservative (safe) compliance estimates
  → workflow continues unblocked

After 4 total graph iterations: CB transitions OPEN → HALF_OPEN
  → one probe allowed (ComplianceAgent gets one more try)
  → success: CB closes
  → failure: CB opens again

docs.title: 8.1 Payload Threading
docs.text:
The initial payload is passed to every node as the default params source:

payload = {
    "company_name": "NovaSemi Technologies",
    "acquirer_name": "GlobalTech Semiconductor Corp",
    "sector": "Semiconductor / Fabless IC Design",
    "raw_data": { "founded": 2014, "employees": 183, ... },
    "deal_context": { "target_revenue_usd_m": 340, ... }
}

Nodes that declare explicit params override the payload. Nodes without params fall back to the full payload dict.

docs.title: 8.2 Context Reference Resolution
docs.text:
Downstream nodes reference upstream outputs using {{steps.<node>.output.<key>}} syntax, resolved before each activity dispatch:

# risk_synthesis params (resolved automatically by _resolve_refs):
financial_analysis:  "{{steps.financial.output}}"
legal_analysis:      "{{steps.legal.output}}"
technical_analysis:  "{{steps.technical.output}}"
market_analysis:     "{{steps.market_primary.output}}"
culture_analysis:    "{{steps.culture.output}}"

After reflection, the context key financial is updated with the improved output — risk_synthesis automatically sees the post-reflection version.

docs.title: 8.3 MongoDB State Documents
docs.text:
Each node's completion writes: 

{
  "workflow_id": "a3f7c82b-...",
  "node_id": "financial",
  "output": {
    "revenue_cagr_3yr": 0.28,
    "ebitda_margin": 0.23,
    "dcf_valuation_usd_m": 485,
    "confidence": 0.87,
    "agent": "FinancialAnalystAgent"
  },
  "updated_at": "2025-03-20T14:32:11.847Z"
}

The collection has a compound index on (workflow_id, node_id) for O(1) point reads.

docs.title: Step 1 — Graph Launch (~0s)
docs.text:
POST /workflows/run
Body: { "dsl": {"steps": [{"name": "due_diligence", "graph": <graph_def>}]}, "payload": {...} }

Response: { "instance_id": "a3f7c82b-4d1e-4892-b7f6-2c8d9e0f1a2b" }

Temporal creates a durable GraphWorkflow execution. The entry node ingest is placed in the pending deque.

docs.title: Step 2 — Data Ingestion (~5–15s)
docs.text:
DataIngestionAgent is called as a Temporal activity with the full payload. The LLM (GPT-4o with JSON mode) normalises the raw company data.

If confidence < 0.70: CritiqueAgent is injected immediately, re-runs data normalisation with stricter instructions.

Expected output: 

{
  "company_name": "NovaSemi Technologies",
  "sector": "Semiconductor / Fabless IC Design",
  "founded_year": 2014,
  "employee_count": 183,
  "hq_jurisdiction": "Texas, USA",
  "revenue_usd_m": [210, 268, 340],
  "key_products": ["NovaCore-V RISC-V SoC", "NovaDrive automotive MCU"],
  "subsidiaries": [],
  "ip_patents": 47,
  "data_quality_issues": ["No audited financials available for 2023"],
  "confidence": 0.86
}

docs.title: Step 3 — Parallel Expert Analysis (~15–45s)
docs.text:
All five analysis edges fire simultaneously. Temporal executes them as 5 concurrent activities.

Expected outputs:

financial: 

{
  "revenue_cagr_3yr": 0.27,
  "ebitda_margin": 0.23,
  "gross_margin": 0.61,
  "net_debt_to_ebitda": 0.4,
  "free_cash_flow_yield": 0.12,
  "dcf_valuation_usd_m": 485,
  "key_financial_risks": ["Customer concentration (top 3 = 71% of revenue)", "No audited 2023 financials"],

}

legal: 

{
  "ip_ownership_clean": true,

  "change_of_control_clauses_count": 4,
  "regulatory_licences_transferable": true,
  "employment_liability_usd_m": 1.2,

  "blocking_issues": [],

}

technical: 

{
  "tech_stack_modernity_score": 8,
  "scalability_rating": 7,
  "technical_debt_severity": "low",
  "security_posture_score": 7,
  "engineering_team_quality": 9,

  "estimated_remediation_cost_usd_m": 3.5,
  "key_technical_risks": ["Legacy verification toolchain", "Single cloud region"],
  "confidence": 0.85
}

market_primary: 

{
  "tam_usd_b": 18.4,
  "sam_usd_b": 3.2,

  "market_growth_cagr_5yr": 0.12,

{

docs.title: Step 4 — Reflection Loops (Automatic)
docs.text:
Culture agent returns confidence=0.79 (< threshold 0.72? No — 0.79 > 0.72, so no reflection). Market agent returns confidence=0.81 (< threshold 0.78? No — 0.81 > 0.78, so no reflection).

If financial returned confidence=0.71 (hypothetical): 

financial__reflect_1 (CritiqueAgent) injected at queue front
→ critique: "DCF model needs explicit terminal value and WACC assumptions"
→ improved_output.confidence = 0.87
→ financial context key updated
→ risk_synthesis sees improved financial data

Reflections triggered metric increments accordingly.

docs.title: Step 5 — Risk Synthesis (~10–20s)
docs.text:
All 5 expert outputs are in context. RiskSynthesizerAgent aggregates.

Expected output: 

{
  "overall_risk_rating": "medium",
  "deal_breakers": [],
  "top_5_risks": [
    {"risk": "Customer concentration (71% HHI)", "severity": "high", "mitigation": "Revenue diversification clause in SPA"},
    {"risk": "18% post-close attrition", "severity": "medium", "mitigation": "$12M retention programme"},
    {"risk": "CFIUS review for defense customer", "severity": "medium", "mitigation": "Engage CFIUS counsel pre-signing"},
    {"risk": "Missing audited 2023 financials", "severity": "medium", "mitigation": "Require Big 4 audit as condition precedent"},
    {"risk": "Legacy verification toolchain", "severity": "low", "mitigation": "Budget $3.5M tech remediation post-close"}
  ],
  "total_risk_adjusted_cost_usd_m": 28.2,
  "recommendation_to_proceed": true,
  "conditions_to_proceed": ["Big 4 audit of FY2023", "CFIUS counsel engagement", "$12M retention programme committed"],
  "confidence": 0.84
}

docs.title: Step 6 — Human Gate (Interrupt)
docs.text:
Workflow pauses. CFO receives alert with risk_synthesis output link.

GET /workflows/{id}/health
→ { "interrupted": true, "pending_count": 1, "cb_trips_total": 0, "errors": [] }

GET /workflows/{id}/state/risk_synthesis
→ risk register output above

CFO decision: APPROVED — resume
POST /workflows/{id}/resume

docs.title: Step 7 — Fan-Out Market Scenarios (~15–30s parallel)
docs.text:
Three MarketAnalysisAgent instances run simultaneously with bear/base/bull assumptions. Highest-confidence result wins:

Variant TAM Growth Moat Confidence Bear case $12B 3% weak 0.74 Base case $18B 8% moderate 0.88 ← selected Bull case $28B 15% strong 0.71

context["market_scenarios"] = base case output.

docs.title: Step 8 — Valuation (~10–20s, with potential cycle)
docs.text:
ValuationAgent receives: financial + risk_synthesis + market_scenarios.

Scenario A — High confidence first run: 

{
  "dcf_value_usd_m": 485,
  "cca_value_usd_m": 510,
  "precedent_tx_value_usd_m": 535,
  "lbo_max_bid_usd_m": 460,
  "recommended_bid_range_usd_m": [470, 525],
  "recommended_premium_pct": 35,
  "ev_to_ebitda_multiple": 6.7,
  "valuation_rationale": "Premium justified by IP portfolio and team quality; discount applied for customer concentration risk",
  "confidence": 0.89
}
→ confidence >= 0.85 → proceeds to compliance (no cycle)

Scenario B — Low confidence triggers cycle: 

valuation.confidence = 0.71 → fires back-edge to financial
financial re-runs (now with risk_synthesis data in context too)
financial__reflect_2 may fire if still low
valuation re-runs → confidence = 0.91 → proceeds

docs.title: Step 9 — Compliance (~10–20s)
docs.text:
{
  "hsr_filing_required": true,
  "eu_phase_1_likely": false,
  "eu_phase_2_risk": false,
  "cfius_review_required": true,
  "sector_specific_approvals": ["US DoD foreign ownership notification"],
  "estimated_clearance_months": 9,
  "remedies_likely_required": false,
  "regulatory_risk_rating": "medium",
  "confidence": 0.86
}

If ComplianceAgent fails 3× (circuit breaker trips): FallbackComplianceAgent returns conservative 18-month estimate with regulatory_risk_rating=high.

docs.title: Step 10 — Executive Summary (~10–20s)
docs.text:
Board memo generated. Reflection fires if confidence < 0.88.

Expected output:

docs.title: 10.1 Execution Metrics (Typical Run)
docs.text: Metric Expected Range Total wall-clock time 45–90 minutes (real LLM) / <30s (demo mode) Nodes executed 11–18 (depends on reflection count) Reflections triggered 0–6 (depends on LLM confidence) Fan-outs executed 1 (market_scenarios) Circuit breaker trips 0 (normal) / 1–3 (degraded) MongoDB documents written 11–18 Temporal activities 11–25 (tools + agents + persist)

docs.title: 10.2 Output Quality Indicators
docs.text: A high-quality run produces: - exec_summary.headline_recommendation ∈ {PROCEED, PROCEED_WITH_CONDITIONS, DO_NOT_PROCEED} - exec_summary.confidence ≥ 0.88 (or reflection fires to improve it) - risk_synthesis.deal_breakers = [] for a viable deal - All confidence scores ≥ respective thresholds (post-reflection) - 0 dead letters

docs.title: 10.3 MCP Tool Call Sequence (Claude perspective)
docs.text:
When operated via Claude through the MCP server, the typical tool call sequence is:

1. multigen_list_agents()              → confirm all agents registered
2. multigen_run_graph(graph_def, payload) → get instance_id
3. [wait loop] multigen_get_metrics(id) → watch nodes_executed climb
4. multigen_get_node_state(id, "risk_synthesis") → read risk output
5. multigen_interrupt(id)              → pause for review
6. multigen_fan_out(id, market_scenarios) → inject bull/base/bear
7. multigen_resume(id)                 → continue
8. [wait] multigen_get_metrics(id)     → confirm valuation done
9. multigen_get_node_state(id, "exec_summary") → read final recommendation
10. multigen_get_state(id)             → read full state for report

docs.title: 11.1 LLM API Timeout
docs.text: Symptom: agent_activity raises openai.Timeout Response: Temporal retry_policy fires (up to 3 attempts, 1s initial backoff) If all retries fail: Circuit breaker records failure; node goes to dead_letters if no fallback

docs.title: 11.2 Invalid JSON from LLM
docs.text: Symptom: json.JSONDecodeError in agent's _llm() helper Response: Agent returns {"raw": "<text>", "confidence": 0.5} — low confidence triggers reflection loop with instruction to format correctly

docs.title: 11.3 OpenAI Rate Limit
docs.text: Symptom: HTTP 429 from OpenAI Response: Temporal exponential backoff (configured in RetryPolicy). All 5 parallel agents share the same rate limit — stagger with initial_interval=timedelta(seconds=2) for production

docs.title: 11.4 ComplianceAgent Failure (Circuit Breaker)
docs.text: Symptom: ComplianceAgent throws 3 consecutive exceptions Response: CB trips to OPEN; FallbackComplianceAgent injected at queue front; returns conservative estimates; workflow continues

docs.title: 11.5 MongoDB Unavailable
docs.text: Symptom: persist_node_state_activity fails Response: Non-fatal — logged as warning. Temporal event history remains authoritative source of truth. MongoDB can be replayed from history when service recovers.

docs.title: 11.6 Worker Crash Mid-Graph
docs.text: Symptom: Temporal worker process dies Response: Temporal detects lost heartbeat; reassigns activities to another worker. GraphWorkflow instance state is replayed from event history — execution continues from exact failure point. BFS queue, context, and circuit breaker state are reconstructed deterministically.

docs.title: 11.7 Low Confidence Loop (Exceeds max_reflections)
docs.text: Symptom: Agent consistently below threshold after max_reflections rounds Response: Reflection budget exhausted; workflow proceeds with best available output; confidence score in final output indicates uncertainty to downstream consumers

docs.title: 12.1 OpenTelemetry Spans
docs.text:
Every node execution produces a span: 

span: graph.node.financial
  graph.workflow_id = "a3f7c82b-..."
  graph.node_id     = "financial"
  graph.agent       = "FinancialAnalystAgent"
  graph.iteration   = 1
  graph.circuit_state = "closed"
  graph.confidence  = 0.83
  duration_ms       = 8420

Configure the OTel exporter via OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT for Jaeger, Tempo, or Datadog.

docs.title: 12.2 Prometheus Metrics
docs.text:
multigen_graph_nodes_total{workflow_id,node_id,status}      # counter
multigen_graph_node_duration_seconds{node_id}               # histogram
multigen_graph_reflections_total{node_id}                   # counter
multigen_graph_circuit_open_total{node_id}                  # counter
multigen_graph_fan_out_total{workflow_id}                   # counter
multigen_graph_errors_total{workflow_id,node_id}            # counter

Scrape from the Prometheus metrics endpoint (configured via METRICS_PORT).

docs.title: 12.3 Live Query API
docs.text:
# Circuit breaker status + errors
curl http://localhost:8000/workflows/{id}/health | jq

# Execution counters
curl http://localhost:8000/workflows/{id}/metrics | jq

docs.title: 12.4 Temporal UI
docs.text: Navigate to http://localhost:8088 to see: - Workflow execution history (every activity + result) - Signal delivery timeline (interrupt, resume, fan_out, etc.) - Child workflow link for nested graph steps - Replay history for debugging

docs.title: 13.1 Installation
docs.text:
Add to Claude Desktop config (~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "multigen": {
      "command": "python",
      "args": ["-m", "mcp_server.server"],
      "cwd": "/path/to/Multigen",
      "env": {
        "MULTIGEN_BASE_URL": "http://localhost:8000"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 13.2 Example Claude Conversation
docs.text:
User: Run M&A due diligence on NovaSemi Technologies being acquired by GlobalTech.
      Revenue $340M, EBITDA $78M, semiconductor sector. They make RISC-V chips.

Claude: I'll run this through Multigen's autonomous due diligence graph.
        [calls multigen_list_agents() → confirms 11 agents registered]
        [calls multigen_run_graph(graph_def=<built graph>, payload=<deal params>)]
        → Workflow started: instance_id = a3f7c82b-...

        I've launched the 11-node due diligence graph. The 5 expert agents
        (financial, legal, technical, market, culture) are now running in parallel.
        I'll monitor and notify you at key milestones.

        [polls multigen_get_metrics() every 30s]
        [nodes_executed reaches 6]

        The parallel expert analysis is complete. Risk synthesis is running.
        [calls multigen_get_node_state(id, "risk_synthesis")]

        Risk synthesis complete. Overall rating: MEDIUM. No deal breakers found.
        Top risk: customer concentration (71% HHI).
        Shall I pause here for your review before valuation, or continue?

User: Pause it. Also run a bear/base/bull market analysis before we continue.

Claude: [calls multigen_interrupt(id)]
        [calls multigen_fan_out(id, {group_id: "scenarios", nodes: [...]})]
        Workflow paused. Running 3 market scenarios in parallel.
        [retrieves fan-out results]
        Base case selected (confidence 0.88): TAM $18B, 8% CAGR, moderate moat.
        Ready to resume when you are.

User: Resume. What's the final recommendation?

Claude: [calls multigen_resume(id)]
        [waits for exec_summary node]
        [calls multigen_get_node_state(id, "exec_summary")]

        RECOMMENDATION: PROCEED WITH CONDITIONS
        Bid range: $470M–$525M (6.7× EV/EBITDA, 20% expected IRR)

        Three conditions before signing:
        1. Big 4 audit of FY2023 financials
        2. CFIUS counsel engagement within 2 weeks
        3. $12M retention programme committed at signing

docs.title: 13.3 Available MCP Tools Summary
docs.text: Tool Description multigen_ping Health check multigen_run_workflow Start DSL or text workflow multigen_run_graph Start complex reasoning graph multigen_get_state Read all node outputs (MongoDB) multigen_get_node_state Read single node output multigen_interrupt Pause at next node boundary multigen_resume Unpause multigen_jump_to Priority-lane a node multigen_skip_node Branch no-go multigen_reroute Add dynamic edge multigen_inject_node Append node at runtime multigen_fan_out Parallel hypothesis testing multigen_prune_branch Cancel branch + descendants multigen_get_health CB status + errors multigen_get_metrics Execution counters multigen_list_agents Available agents multigen_list_capabilities Capability directory multigen_register_capability Register new agent

docs.title: 15.1 Latency Budget (Real LLM, GPT-4o)
docs.text:
Phase Nodes Parallelism Latency Data ingestion 1 Sequential 5–15s Expert analysis 5 Full parallel 15–45s Reflection (if triggered) 1–6 Sequential 8–20s each Risk synthesis 1 Sequential 10–20s Fan-out market 3 Full parallel 15–25s Valuation 1 Sequential 10–20s Compliance 1 Sequential 8–15s Executive summary 1 Sequential 12–20s Total (no reflection) ~75–160s Total (max reflection) ~180–360s

vs. Traditional due diligence: 2–12 weeks

docs.title: 15.2 Scaling
docs.text:
• More parallel agents: Add nodes with edges from ingest, no code changes
  • Higher throughput: Scale temporal_worker replicas; each handles independent workflow instances
  • Faster LLM: Use gpt-4o-mini for lower-stakes nodes (culture, market variants); gpt-4o for financial/legal/exec_summary
  • Caching: Identical ingestion runs can short-circuit via MongoDB lookup before activity dispatch

docs.title: 16.1 Add a Sector-Specialist Agent (Dynamic Blueprint)
docs.text:
For a semiconductor deal, inject a dynamic RISC-V specialist:

await client.inject_node(wf_id, InjectNodeRequest(
    id="risc_v_specialist",
    blueprint={
        "system_prompt": "You are a RISC-V IP licensing expert with 15 years in semiconductor M&A.",
        "instruction": "Assess the RISC-V patent portfolio value and freedom-to-operate risk.",
    },
    edges_to=["risk_synthesis"],
    reflection_threshold=0.80,
))

The agent is created at runtime — no deployment needed.

docs.title: 16.2 Add a Second Human Gate (Board Vote)
docs.text:
After exec_summary: 

await client.interrupt(wf_id)          # pause after exec_summary
# Board reviews and votes
await client.reroute(wf_id,            # add dynamic edge based on vote outcome
    source="exec_summary",
    target="term_sheet_drafter",       # new node injected separately
    condition="",
)
await client.resume(wf_id)

docs.title: 16.3 Multi-Deal Portfolio Screening
docs.text:
Run 10 deals in parallel (independent Temporal workflow instances):

deals = [("CompanyA", ...), ("CompanyB", ...), ...]
tasks = [client.run_workflow(dsl=build_dsl(d), payload=build_payload(d)) for d in deals]
responses = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
# All 10 graphs run concurrently across the worker pool

docs.title: 16.4 Continuous Monitoring (Post-Acquisition)
docs.text:
After close, repurpose the graph as a recurring integration health monitor: - Replace FinancialAnalystAgent with IntegrationProgressAgent - Replace CritiqueAgent with MilestoneReviewAgent - Add a loop step: loop(until="integration_score >= 0.95", max_iterations=24) (monthly runs)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
A multi-stage research synthesis pipeline that gathers sources, summarises and fact-checks in parallel, iteratively improves quality through a critique loop, and produces a structured research report.

Research topic demonstrated: "Impact of Large Language Models on Software Development Productivity"

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
TopicExtractor
      │
SourceSearcher (mock: Semantic Scholar / arXiv in production)
      │
   ┌──┴──┐  (MapReduce parallel)
   ▼     ▼
Summarize  FactCheck
   └──┬──┘  (reducer: QualityCritic)
      │
QualityCritic ◄──────────────────────┐
      │                               │
      │ quality < 0.75?               │ revise loop
      ▼                               │
ReportGenerator ─────────────────────┘

docs.title: Key Agents
docs.text: Agent Role Real Implementation TopicExtractor Decompose research question into sub-topics GPT-4o structured output SourceSearcher Retrieve relevant literature Semantic Scholar API + vector DB SummarySynthesizer Write coherent narrative synthesis GPT-4o synthesis prompt FactChecker Cross-check claims against sources GPT-4o with source context QualityCritic Score synthesis quality GPT-4o as judge (0–1 score) ReportGenerator Produce final structured report GPT-4o report template

docs.title: Self-Correction Loop
docs.text:
The pipeline uses an iterative refinement state machine:

for iteration in range(max_iterations):
    # Run parallel summarise + fact-check
    parallel_analysis.run(goal, inputs)

    quality = context.scratch["critique"]["overall_quality"]
    if quality >= 0.75:
        break  # quality threshold met

    # Accumulate critique in EpisodicMemory for next iteration
    memory["critique_iter_{i}"] = critique

Quality improves each iteration as the pipeline incorporates critique feedback. EpisodicMemory ensures the improving context is available across iterations.

docs.title: Ensemble Confidence Score
docs.text:
Run the pipeline N times to measure output stability:

agreement_score = 1.0 - (std_quality / mean_quality)

High agreement (> 0.90) indicates the pipeline produces consistent results regardless of LLM sampling randomness. Low agreement suggests the research topic is genuinely ambiguous or sources are contradictory.
